title: Only while, do, for and switch statements should be labelled
description: Only "while", "do", "for" and "switch" statements should be labelledAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoLabels allow specifying a target statement to jump to using thebreakorcontinuestatements. It’s possible to assign a label to any statement or block of statements. However, using it with any statement can create a complex control flow path, making the code harder to understand and maintain.myLabel: if (i % 2 == 0) { // Noncompliant: Labeling an if statement if (i == 12) { console.log("12"); break myLabel; } console.log("Even number, but not 12"); }Instead of using a label with these nestedifstatements, this code block should be refactored.if (i % 2 == 0) { // Compliant if (i == 12) { console.log("12"); } else { console.log("Ev
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not if statements
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not try statements
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not function declarations
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not block statements
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not function calls
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not return statements
Labels should only be used with while, do, for, and switch statements - not throw statements
